radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ with s/p CVA, p/w acute respiratory distress, dysphagia,
significant upper airiway secretions; intubated for respiratory distress,
hypoxia;  // eval for mechanical obstruction, pna, upper airway inflammation

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 284 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT torso ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  Moderate atherosclerotic calcifications seen throughout
the thoracic aorta.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are within
normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is seen.  No mediastinal mass.  The esophagus is significantly
patulous and filled with solid and liquid material, more solid at the inferior
esophagus just superior to the GE junction.

PLEURAL SPACES:  There are likely trace pleural effusions bilaterally.  No
pneumothorax.


LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  The endotracheal tube tip is approximately 2.7 cm above the
carina.  There are secretions along the superior portion of the ET tube. 
There are diffuse hazy nodular with some ground-glass opacities in the upper
lobes bilaterally.  There are dense consolidative opacities in the lower lobes
bilaterally.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck demonstrate
heterogeneous appearance of the thyroid

ABDOMEN:  There is a moderate hiatal hernia.  The remainder of the imaged
upper abdomen is unremarkable besides tiny hypodensity at the upper pole the
right kidney which is too small to characterize but likely a cyst.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.
Scoliosis is similar to prior.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2. Significantly patulous esophagus is distended with liquid and solid
material, with more solid components just proximal to the GE junction,
suggesting a component of mechanical and/or functional partial obstruction.
3. Dense consolidations in the lower lobes bilaterally likely represent a
combination of atelectasis, aspiration, and infection.  Diffuse hazy nodular
opacities in the upper lobes bilaterally are likely due to infection and/or
inflammation.
4. There are secretions along the superior portion of the ET tube.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Hepatocellular carcinoma, for restaging; status of disease and
chemotherapy.

COMPARISONS:  CT abdomen and pelvis dated ___ torso CT dated ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT images from the thoracic inlet to the ischial tuberosities
were obtained both without and with intravenous contrast in multiple phases.
Oral contrast was also administered.  Multiplanar reformations were essential
to interpretation.

CHEST:  Bilateral upper lobe subpleural blebs are present.  There is no
evidence of pulmonary nodule, mass, or consolidation.  There are no
pathologically enlarged axillary, hilar, or mediastinal lymph nodes.  The
heart and great vessels appear unremarkable.  There is no pleural or
pericardial effusion.

ABDOMEN:  There is redemonstration of multiple subtle arterially enhancing
subcentimeter foci, which are predominantly peripheral in location.  There is
no definite associated accelerated washout.  There is also redemonstration of
a large ill-defined heterogeneously hypodense mass, which measures
approximately 7.3 x 9.6 cm, compared to 7.4 x 10.6 cm on the prior study.  The
double-J stent is in stable position originating at the common duct and
terminating in the duodenum.  The hepatic mass causes intrahepatic biliary
ductal dilatation, which is mild and similar when compared to the prior study.
There is redemonstration of expansion of the right portal vein by tumor.  The
tumor also invades the left and middle hepatic veins.  However, there is no
evidence of extension into the inferior vena cava or right atrium.  Dystrophic
calcifications within the mass are again identified.  There is a second
hypodense focus in segment VI, which measures 11 x 12 mm and is essentially
unchanged when compared to the previous study.  The previously described
omental stranding is less apparent on the current study.

The spleen, pancreas, adrenal glands, and kidneys are within normal limits.
There has been no significant interval change in scattered subcentimeter porta
hepatis and celiac axis lymph nodes, which measure up to 8 mm in short axis.
The abdominal aorta demonstrates a normal caliber.  There is no evidence of
bowel dilatation or ascites.

PELVIS:  Trace free pelvic fluid is present.  The rectum, sigmoid colon,
urinary bladder, prostate gland, and seminal vesicles appear grossly
unremarkable.  The prostate gland is noted to be mildly enlarged at 5.3 cm in
transverse dimension.  There is no evidence of bowel dilatation or lymph node
enlargement.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or blastic lesions are identified.
There is a sclerotic focus in the right iliac bone, which is stable and most
compatible with a bone island.  There is a stable deformity of the right
inferior pubic ramus at its junction with the symphysis, compatible with a
remote fracture.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Slight interval decrease in size of a large necrotic hepatic mass, with
involvement of both the portal venous and hepatic venous systems.

2.  Multiple subtle arterially enhancing foci, nonspecific findings which may
represent perfusion abnormalities.  Attention is recommended on followup
imaging.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with anorexia and pleural effusion with history
of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma.

COMPARISON:  CT torso, ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Contrast-enhanced axial images of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
were obtained with multiplanar reformatted images.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  There are no pathologically enlarged axillary lymph
nodes.  Scattered calcifications associated with hypoattenuating foci in the
left thyroid lobe do not appear significantly changed. The right thyroid lobe
may be surgically absent. There is multichamber cardiac enlargements with
mitral annular, mitral valve, and aortic valve calcifications. There is
significant myocardial thinning at the cardiac apex, likely related to
ischemia, without definite aneurysmal dilatation. There is no significant
pericardial effusion. Atherosclerotic calcification of the coronary arteries
and the thoracic aorta is observed.  A precarinal lymph node measures 16 x 10
mm, previously 14 x 12 mm.

There has been significant increase in size of a now large right pleural
effusion and development of a new small left pleural effusion.  The nodular
soft tissue density along the periphery of the right lower lobe fissure seen
in ___ is less conspicuous today.  No new lung nodules are identified. The
airways appear patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally, though scattered
bronchial calcifications are noted.

CT ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST: A few subcentimeter hypoattenuating right hepatic
lesions (2:58 and 52) are too small to characterize and not definitively
identified on prior studies. The gallbladder is mildly distended and contains
a few gallstones within. The spleen, pancreas and adrenal glands appear
unremarkable.  The kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast normally
and there is no hydronephrosis or hydroureter.  A 15-mm interpolar left renal
cyst is unchanged.  Other scattered bilateral hypoattenuating renal lesions
are too small to characterize.  Intra-abdominal loops of large and small bowel
are unremarkable and there is no free air, free fluid or pathologically
enlarged mesenteric lymph nodes.  Scattered retroperitoneal lymph nodes do not
meet CT criteria for pathologic enlargement.  The abdominal aorta is
atherosclerotic and tortuous as are its branches.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  Evaluation of the pelvis is limited due to right hip
prosthesis.  The rectum and bladder appear unremarkable.  A Foley is present
within the bladder.  The sigmoid colon contains diverticula without evidence
of acute diverticulitis.  Calcifications within the uterus likely represent
fibroids.  No pathologically enlarged pelvic lymph nodes or free fluid is
identified.  Tiny scattered anterior subcutaneous pockets of air likely
related to injections.

Bone windows reveal osteopenia and severe multilevel thoracolumbar
degenerative changes with fusion of the L3, L4 and L5 vertebral bodies.
Thoracolumbar scoliosis, moderate, is also present.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence for new malignancy or recurrent lymphoma.

2.  Large right and small left pleural effusion.

3.  Multichamber cardiac enlargement with evidence of prior apical ischemia. 

4.  Cholelithiasis

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man with vascular mass on the back.

No comparison is available.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial MDCT images of the chest, abdomen and pelvis were obtained
after administration of 130 cc of Optiray intravenously.  Sagittal and coronal
reformatted images were then acquired.

CT OF THE CHEST:  Necrotic nodes are located in the left hilar region.  The
largest one measures 22 x 21 mm.  A 20 x 17 mm left axillary node is also
visualized.  A large necrotic mass is located within the left lower lobe
measuring 49 x 48 mm.  There is adjacent atelectasis and vascular congestion
near the mass.  There is an 18 x 18 mm enhancing nodule within the left
pleural cavity.  Small bilateral pleural effusions are noted, left greater
than right.   Multiple sub-5-mm pulmonary nodules are noted within the left
upper lobe.  5-mm ground-glass nodules are also noted within the right upper
lobe. 

Diffuse calcification of the aorta and coronary arteries are noted.  Severe
calcification of the mitral valve is also noted.  No pericardial effusion is
seen.  A large posterior midline mass is noted adjacent to the T2 thoracic
vertebra in the subcutaneous tissue measuring approximately 4.2 x 5.7 cm.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  The right adrenal gland contains a mass
measuring 3.5 x 3.9 cm.  The left adrenal gland is normal.  The kidneys,
spleen, liver, and the pancreas have normal appearance.  Cholelithiasis with
no evidence of cholecystitis is noted.  The stomach, duodenum, loops of small
bowel and large bowel and appendix are normal.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The urinary bladder, distal ureters,
rectum, sigmoid colon, and seminal vesicles appear normal.  Prostate gland
contains multiple areas of calcification.  No free air or fluid is noted
within the abdomen and pelvis.  No pathologically enlarged nodes are noted
within the abdomen and pelvis.

BONE WINDOWS:  No concerning lytic or sclerotic lesions are identified.

IMPRESSION:  
1.  Left lower lobe lung mass is associated with multiple left hilar necrotic
nodes and left axillary node. Left pleural mass, right adrenal mass and a
subcutaneous mass within the upper thoracic region are noted. Findings are
compatible with metastatic disease most likely from primary lung cancer.
Metastatic melanoma is another likely possiblity.

2.  Small bilateral pleural effusions.

3.  Cholelithiasis with no evidence of cholecystitis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CLINICAL HISTORY:  ___ man with cough and dyspnea and chest radiograph
concerning for pneumonia and effusion.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___ and chest radiograph ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric multidetector CT acquisition of the chest was performed
without intravenous or oral contrast.  Images are presented for display in the
axial plane as 5-mm and 1.25-mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar
reformation images were submitted for review.

CT CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: No nodules are seen in the unenhanced
thyroid gland.  Enlargement of the ascending thoracic aorta to 4.2 cm is
unchanged from ___ with mild atherosclerotic calcifications throughout
its course.  The pulmonary artery is of normal caliber.  No pathologically
enlarged axillary lymph nodes are identified.  An enlarged lymph node in the
in the right paratracheal station (2:18) measures 18 x 17 mm, increased in
size from ___ when it measured 15 x 16 mm.  Multiple other lymph nodes
in the paratracheal and pre-vascular stations are stable. Evaluation of hilar
lymphadenopathy is limited by lack of intravenous contrast.  Coronary artery
calcifications are seen in the LAD, right coronary artery and left circumflex
as well as the aortic annulus.  A trace pericardial effusion is increased from
___.  Atherosclerotic calcifications are seen at the origin of the
great vessels.  There are bilateral non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions, similar
in size to ___.

Lung window images demonstrate peribronchial consolidative and ground-glass
opacities in the bilateral lower lobes left greater than right, worse than on
___, likely representing a combination of atelectasis and asymmetric
edema, but underlying infection cannot be excluded. In addition, there are
ground-glass opacities in the bilateral upper lobes, in part due to
respiration, with other areas of more confluent opacity, improved from
___.  Central airways are patent.  A sub-4-mm nodule in the right
middle lobe is newly apparent from ___ (2:42).

ABDOMEN:  The study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation.  The
patient is status post cholecystectomy.  The visualized portions of the
unenhanced liver, spleen, and kidneys are unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic
calcifications are seen at the origin of the celiac and SMA arteries.  The
adrenal glands are normal. A small hiatal hernia is unchanged.

BONE WINDOWS:  No bone finding suspicious for infection or malignancy is seen.
Multilevel degenerative change with scoliotic deformity of the spine is
similar in appearance to ___.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Bilateral lower lobe consolidations are worse than on ___ and are
likely a combination of atelectasis and mild asymmetric pulmonary edema.
Underlying infection cannot be excluded and follow up is recommended after
treatment. Peribronchial ground-glass opacities with some areas of more
confluent consolidation in the upper lobes are improved from ___.
Moderate bilateral pleural effusions are similar in size to ___.
2.  Multiple prominent mediastinal lymph nodes, stable to minimally increased
from ___ may be reactive.
3.  Unchanged ectasia of the aortic root to 4.2 cm without aneurysmal
dilation.

sections.technique:
Volumetric multidetector CT acquisition of the chest was performed
without intravenous or oral contrast.  Images are presented for display in the
axial plane as 5-mm and 1.25-mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar
reformation images were submitted for review.

CT CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: No nodules are seen in the unenhanced
thyroid gland.  Enlargement of the ascending thoracic aorta to 4.2 cm is
unchanged from ___ with mild atherosclerotic calcifications throughout
its course.  The pulmonary artery is of normal caliber.  No pathologically
enlarged axillary lymph nodes are identified.  An enlarged lymph node in the
in the right paratracheal station (2:18) measures 18 x 17 mm, increased in
size from ___ when it measured 15 x 16 mm.  Multiple other lymph nodes
in the paratracheal and pre-vascular stations are stable. Evaluation of hilar
lymphadenopathy is limited by lack of intravenous contrast.  Coronary artery
calcifications are seen in the LAD, right coronary artery and left circumflex
as well as the aortic annulus.  A trace pericardial effusion is increased from
___.  Atherosclerotic calcifications are seen at the origin of the
great vessels.  There are bilateral non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions, similar
in size to ___.

Lung window images demonstrate peribronchial consolidative and ground-glass
opacities in the bilateral lower lobes left greater than right, worse than on
___, likely representing a combination of atelectasis and asymmetric
edema, but underlying infection cannot be excluded. In addition, there are
ground-glass opacities in the bilateral upper lobes, in part due to
respiration, with other areas of more confluent opacity, improved from
___.  Central airways are patent.  A sub-4-mm nodule in the right
middle lobe is newly apparent from ___ (2:42).

ABDOMEN:  The study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation.  The
patient is status post cholecystectomy.  The visualized portions of the
unenhanced liver, spleen, and kidneys are unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic
calcifications are seen at the origin of the celiac and SMA arteries.  The
adrenal glands are normal. A small hiatal hernia is unchanged.

BONE WINDOWS:  No bone finding suspicious for infection or malignancy is seen.
Multilevel degenerative change with scoliotic deformity of the spine is
similar in appearance to ___.
sections.impression:
1.  Bilateral lower lobe consolidations are worse than on ___ and are
likely a combination of atelectasis and mild asymmetric pulmonary edema.
Underlying infection cannot be excluded and follow up is recommended after
treatment. Peribronchial ground-glass opacities with some areas of more
confluent consolidation in the upper lobes are improved from ___.
Moderate bilateral pleural effusions are similar in size to ___.
2.  Multiple prominent mediastinal lymph nodes, stable to minimally increased
from ___ may be reactive.
3.  Unchanged ectasia of the aortic root to 4.2 cm without aneurysmal
dilation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Ulcerative colitis flare, evaluate for underlying infection.

No priors are available.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the chest without
contrast.  5-mm and 1.25-mm axial images as well as coronal/sagittal
reformations were evaluated.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST:  Within the apical medial right upper lobe,
there is a cluster of nonspecific nodular opacities (4:32 through 47).  The
remaining lungs are clear and the airways are patent to the subsegmental
level.  There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  No pathologically
enlarged axillary or mediastinal lymph nodes are present.  Residual thymic
tissue is noted within the anterior mediastinum.

Included portions of the upper abdomen display abnormal loss of haustral folds
and wall edema involving the splenic flexure with no pneumoperitoneum or
additional intra-abdominal abnormality is present.  No aggressive-appearing
osseous lesions are noted.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Nonspecific clustering of small opacities at right apex medially, likely
infectious or inflammatory.  Follow-up CXR may be helpful to exclude an
early/developing infection at this site.

2.  Partially visualized sequelae of ulcerative colitis with loss of haustral
folds and wall edema involving the splenic flexure.  This can be better
assessed with a dedicated CT abdomen and pelvis exam.

Findings were discussed with Dr. ___ on date of exam at 16:30 p.m.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female presenting from hemodialysis where patient
displayed altered mental status but normal vital signs.  The patient has a
known history of diabetes, breast cancer, pulmonary sarcoid, unilateral
oophorectomy and ventral hernia.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT CT acquired axial images were obtained through the chest,
abdomen, and pelvis without intravenous or oral contrast.  Coronal and
sagittal reformations were evaluated.

Comparison is made to most recent CT examination dated ___ and
chest CT from ___

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  Multiple prominent axillary
lymph nodes are identified bilaterally in addition to hilar lymphadenopathy. A
left- sided central venous catheter is noted to be in place. Atherosclerotic
disease is noted within the aorta and coronary circulation. The lungs display
small bilateral simple pleural effusions (right greater than left) with
adjacent compression atelectasis and scattered regions of linear scarring and
atelectasis. Scattered nodular opacities are noted adjacent to the hila within
the upper lobes bilaterally.  Diffuse ground glass opacity may relate to air
trapping or pulmonary edema.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS OR ORAL CONTRAST:  Non-contrast
evaluation of the liver is unremarkable.  There is stable appearance of
cholelithiasis without evidence of acute cholecystitis.  The spleen, stomach,
small bowel, pancreas, adrenal glands, and atrophic-appearing kidneys are
unremarkable.  Extensive vascular atherosclerotic disease is again noted.  A
large ventral hernia containing non-dilated small-bowel loops is unchanged
with moderate amount of fluid again noted inferiorly within the left-sided
portion of the patient's pannus. No CT findings to suggest incarceration are
identified. Mild mesenteric stranding is redemonstrated.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS OR ORAL CONTRAST:  Intrapelvic bowel
displays mild diverticulosis but no evidence of acute diverticulitis.  The
appendix is normal.  No abnormal free fluid is identified within the pelvic
cavity.  Urinary bladder is unremarkable.

BONES/SOFT TISSUES:  There has been extensive worsening in the degree of
anasarca within the soft tissues from prior examination.  No malignant-
appearing osseous lesions are identified.  No significant degenerative changes
are noted within the spine.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No etiology for patient's altered mental status identified.

2.  Interval development of small bilateral pleural effusions and extensive
worsening of diffuse anasarca.

3.  Extensive bilateral nodular opacities predominantly within the upper lobes
may be sequela of the patient's known sarcoidosis or represent an acute
infectious process.  Scattered ground-glass opacities, resulting in mosaic
attenuation in the lung is likely related to underlying airtrapping or mild
alveolar edema.  Would consider repeat chest CT when patient is stable to
better stage sarcoid if alteration in management will occur.

4.  Unchanged large ventral hernia without CT findings to suggest
incarceration/strangulation although this is a clinical diagnosis.

5. Cholelithiasis.
